steps.title: Rewrite the exponent
steps.content:
A power of $\frac{1}{2}$ means the principal square root. Since $35z$ is positive when $z>1$, the expression can be rewritten directly as a square root.
$$(35z)^{\frac{1}{2}}=\sqrt{35z}$$

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content: Choice A multiplies by $35$ outside the square root, which would be much larger. Choice B puts only $35$ under the square root and leaves $z$ outside. Choice D squares the original quantity before taking the square root. The expression from step 1 matches choice C. The answer is C.
